query: How frequently in 2026 has Russia launched overnight drone barrages against Ukraine exceeding 600 drones in a single night? List specific dates and drone counts for the largest barrages in May, June, and early July 2026 according to Ukrainian Air Force figures.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Summary

**Critical correction:** The famous "record 728 Shahed drones / 741 total aerial weapons" barrage on Lutsk was launched **July 8–9, 2025 — not 2026**. Sources with 2025 datelines (Euronews, Kyiv Independent, Kyiv Post) confirm this. It should not be attributed to 2026.

**2026 pattern — peak in May, then de-escalation:**

Nights exceeding ~600 UAVs in 2026 (Ukrainian Air Force figures via ISIS), roughly **6 confirmed**, concentrated almost entirely in May:

- **May 1:** ~619 UAVs
- **May 13–14 (overnight):** ~892 UAVs — the largest single-night barrage of 2026 (part of a ~38-hour, ~1,567-drone operation, the war's largest to that point)
- **May 14:** ~675 UAVs
- **May 24:** ~600 UAVs
- **June 1–2:** ~656 drones + 73 missiles = 729 total (largest June night)
- **June 15:** ~611 UAVs

**Early July 2026 — notable de-escalation, no night exceeded 600 drones:**
- July 1–2: 496 drones + 74 missiles (570 total)
- July 4: ~86 drones
- July 8–9: ~94 drones

An important caveat: many high totals include large decoy fractions (Gerbera/Parodiya); pure Shahed/Geran strike-drone counts typically ran 380–570 per night. The largest 2026 single overnight barrage was ~892 UAVs (May 13–14). Full structured findings with all data points and sources have been recorded.

sources.title: Monthly Analysis of Russian Shahed 136 Deployment Against Ukraine (June 2026)
sources.snippet: Compiles Ukrainian Air Force nightly figures; May 13 ~892 UAVs single night; May/June nights >600; June total 5,749 Shahed-type UAVs.

sources.title: 14 May 2026 Russian strikes on Ukraine
sources.snippet: Night of 13-14 May 2026: largest aerial attack since invasion; 1,567 drones and 56 missiles per Zelensky; 27 killed.

sources.title: Russia attacks Ukraine with 570 drones and missiles overnight (1-2 July 2026)
sources.snippet: July 1-2 2026: 570 total aerial targets = 496 drones + 74 missiles per Ukrainian Air Force.

sources.title: Russia launches missiles and 86 drones against Ukraine (4 July 2026)
sources.snippet: July 4 2026: 2 missiles + 86 strike drones per Ukrainian Armed Forces.

sources.title: Russia attacks Ukraine with two missiles and nearly 100 drones (9 July 2026)
sources.snippet: July 8-9 2026: 2 ballistic missiles + 94 drones; 72 drones downed.

sources.title: Russia launches 729 drones and missiles at Ukraine (June 1-2 2026)
sources.snippet: June 1-2 2026: 656 attack drones + 73 missiles = 729 total aerial weapons per Ukrainian Air Force.

sources.title: Ukraine Faces Record Air Assault: 741 Drones and Missiles Launched, Lutsk Hit Hardest
sources.snippet: Record 741 aerial weapons including 728 Shahed-type drones on Lutsk — this event dates to July 8-9, 2025 (per corroborating 2025-dated Euronews/Kyiv Independent URLs).

sources.title: Russia launches record 741 drones and missiles in its largest aerial attack against Ukraine
sources.snippet: 2025/07/09-dated URL confirming the 728/741 Lutsk record was July 2025, not 2026.

sources.title: Russia launches over 800 drones in daytime mass attack on Ukraine (May 13 2026)
sources.snippet: May 13 2026 daytime mass attack exceeded 800 drones; 14 killed, 80+ injured.

sources.title: Russia Launches Record 1,600-Drone and Missile Attack on Ukraine
sources.snippet: May 13-14 2026 described as largest and longest aerial assault since invasion, 1,600+ drones/missiles over ~30-38 hours.

key_facts: The record 728 Shahed-drone / 741-total-aerial-weapon attack on Lutsk was July 8-9, 2025 — NOT 2026 (Euronews, Kyiv Independent, Kyiv Post 2025 datelines).
2026's heaviest barrages occurred in May; the single largest 2026 overnight barrage was ~892 UAVs on the night of May 13-14, 2026 (ISIS estimate of Ukrainian Air Force figures).
The May 13-14, 2026 assault was the war's largest to that point: a ~38-hour operation totaling 1,567 drones + 56 missiles per President Zelensky.
May 2026 nights exceeding ~600 UAVs: May 1 (~619), May 13-14 overnight (~892), May 14 (~675), May 24 (~600).
June 2026 largest night was June 1-2: ~656 attack drones + 73 missiles = 729 total aerial weapons (RBC-Ukraine citing Air Force); June 15 was ~611 UAVs.
Early July 2026 saw a MARKED DE-ESCALATION: July 1-2 was 496 drones + 74 missiles (570 total, below 600 drones); July 4 was only ~86 drones; July 8-9 was ~94 drones.
Roughly 6 nights in 2026 (through July 13) exceeded ~600 total UAVs, concentrated almost entirely in May.
Many high totals include large decoy-drone fractions (Gerbera/Parodiya); pure Shahed/Geran strike-drone counts typically ran 380-570 per night.
ISIS reported Russia launched 5,749 Shahed-type UAVs in June 2026, describing a break from the earlier rapid-growth pattern seen January-May.

data_points.metric: Russian UAVs launched overnight May 13-14 2026 (single-night portion)
Russian Shahed-type UAVs launched during June 2026 (whole month)
